I open the failed IDoc and check the Status Records section at the bottom — this contains the exact error message that explains why processing failed, such as missing master data or invalid partner configuration. Based on the error message, I fix the underlying issue — create missing master data, extend the material to the required plant, or correct partner profile settings in WE20. Once fixed, I use WE19 to reprocess the IDoc. For recurring failures, I analyze the pattern — wrong data from external system, or missing master data that needs to be maintained.
support-debugging-006.interviewScriptHinglish: ek IDoc failure troubleshoot karne ke liye, main WE02 se shuru karta hoon, direction, message type aur date range ke filters apply karta hoon, phir error status se filter karta hoon - typically application errors ke liye status 51. Main failed IDoc open karta hoon aur niche Status Records section check karta hoon - isme exact error message hota hai jo explain karta hai processing kyun fail hui, jaise missing master data ya invalid partner configuration. Error message ke basis pe, main underlying issue fix karta hoon - missing master data create karta hoon, material ko required plant pe extend karta hoon, ya WE20 mein partner profile settings correct karta hoon. Fix hone ke baad, main WE19 use karke IDoc reprocess karta hoon. Recurring failures ke liye, main pattern analyze karta hoon - external system se wrong data, ya missing master data jo maintain karni hai.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Deleting a status 51 IDoc without reprocessing — the original business transaction (PO, GR, invoice) is then lost.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always reprocess after fixing the root cause, never delete IDoc records unless explicitly confirmed as duplicate.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Using WE19 to reprocess hundreds of IDocs one by one — very time-consuming.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: After verifying the fix with one IDoc via WE19, use BD87 for bulk reprocessing.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Guessing the processing FM name or searching by keyword — use WE57 or WE64 which are the official configuration tables.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Wrong FM identification wastes debugging time.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Modifying production IDoc data in WE19 without understanding why the original data was wrong — the external system will send the same wrong data again.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Fix the source, not just the symptom.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: IDoc list display — filter by date, status, partner; main triage screen for failed inbound/outbound IDocs.
IDoc test tool — copy an IDoc, modify segments, and repost without disturbing the original.
Status code maintenance — lookup table for IDoc status numbers like 51 application error, 53 posted OK.
IDoc control record table — header info: direction, partner, message type, status.
IDoc data segments table — actual payload rows for each IDoc.
Application error on inbound IDoc — posting program rejected the data; root cause in the status text.
Application document posted — successful inbound processing; nothing more needed.
Function module that returns control, data and status records of an IDoc for programmatic checks.
Structure of status records returned by IDOC_READ_COMPLETELY — contains status code and message text.
WE19/BD87 action that re-triggers inbound processing of an IDoc after the root cause is fixed.

support-debugging-006.quickRevisionNotes:
What: IDoc support uses WE02/WE19/BD87 to monitor, modify and repost intermediate documents that move data between SAP and other systems.

Used for:
- Triaging failed inbound IDocs in status 51 by reading the status text and source segment.
- Editing and reposting fixed IDocs via WE19 without losing the original.
- Tracking outbound IDocs through statuses 03 (sent) and partner failures.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- Tcodes: WE02 display, WE05 monitor, WE19 test/repost, BD87 mass repost, WE47 status texts.
- Tables: EDIDC (control), EDID4 (segments), EDIDS (status history).
- FM: IDOC_READ_COMPLETELY for programmatic status checks.

Interview keyword: IDoc debugging starts at status code, then segment data, then partner profile.

followupAnswerBank.question: What does IDoc status 51 mean and how do you fix it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: IDoc status 51 Application Error indicate karta hai — IDoc SAP system ne receive kiya lekin application posting fail hua. IDoc syntactically valid hai lekin aisa data contain karta hai jo posting function module process nahi kar saka. Main WE02 mein failed IDoc open karta hu, niche Status Records section tak scroll karta hu, aur error message padhta hu — ye directly cause batata hai, jaise ek plant mein missing material, invalid vendor account, ya wrong document type. Underlying master data ya configuration issue fix karne ke baad, main IDoc reprocess karne ke liye WE19 use karta hu aur verify karta hu ki status 53 (Successfully Posted) mein change hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: IDoc status 51 indicates an Application Error — the IDoc was received by the SAP system but the application posting failed. The IDoc is syntactically valid but contains data that the posting function module could not process. I open the failed IDoc in WE02, scroll to the Status Records section at the bottom, and read the error message — it directly states the cause, such as a missing material in a plant, invalid vendor account, or wrong document type. After fixing the underlying master data or configuration issue, I use WE19 to reprocess the IDoc and verify that status changes to 53 (Successfully Posted).
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Status 51 ke 80% cases mein master data issue hota hai — material not extended to plant, customer not maintained, or company code configuration missing. Functional team ke saath coordinate karo master data fix ke liye, phir WE19 se reprocess karo.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Deleting a status 51 IDoc without reprocessing — the original business transaction (PO, GR, invoice) is then lost. Always reprocess after fixing the root cause, never delete IDoc records unless explicitly confirmed as duplicate.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WE02 navigation for status 51:
" WE02 → Direction: 2 (Inbound) → Status: 51 → Execute
" Click failed IDoc → scroll to bottom → Status Records section
" Status | Date | Time | Text
"  51   | today | 09:15 | Material 100234 not maintained in plant 1000

" Fix: MM50 → extend material to plant 1000
" Or: MM01 → create material with plant data

" Reprocess via WE19:
" WE19 → IDoc number → Execute
" Standard task: select processing FM → Execute
" Result: status should change to 53 (posted) or 51 again

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between WE02, WE19, BD87, and WE20?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: WE02 IDoc monitoring transaction hai — IDocs ko status, message type, partner, aur date se find, filter, aur display karne ke liye use hota hai. WE19 test aur reprocessing tool hai — underlying issue fix karne ke baad single IDoc repost karne ke liye use hota hai, aur repost karne se pehle data modification allow karta hai. BD87 mass IDoc reprocessing ke liye hai — jab dozens ya hundreds IDocs mein same fixable issue ho, BD87 unhe ek run mein reprocess karta hai. WE20 partner profiles manage karta hai — define karta hai har partner kaunse message types bhej ya receive kar sakta hai aur kaunsa port aur processing FM use karna hai. WE47 IDoc status code meanings ke liye reference hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: WE02 is the IDoc monitoring transaction — used to find, filter, and display IDocs by status, message type, partner, and date. WE19 is the test and reprocessing tool — used to repost a single IDoc after fixing the underlying issue, and allows data modification before reposting. BD87 is for mass IDoc reprocessing — when dozens or hundreds of IDocs have the same fixable issue, BD87 reprocesses them all in one run. WE20 manages partner profiles — defines which message types each partner can send or receive and which port and processing FM to use. WE47 is the reference for IDoc status code meanings.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Daily support workflow: WE02 mein check karo failed IDocs (status 51 or 56) → root cause fix karo → ek IDoc WE19 se test karo → agar fix kaam kiya → BD87 se remaining sab reprocess karo. Yeh efficient batch approach hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using WE19 to reprocess hundreds of IDocs one by one — very time-consuming. After verifying the fix with one IDoc via WE19, use BD87 for bulk reprocessing.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transaction summary:
" WE02 → IDoc list monitoring, filter by status/type/partner/date
" WE19 → single IDoc test/reprocess/modify before repost
" BD87 → mass reprocessing of multiple failed IDocs
"         BD87 → select status 51 → enter date range → Execute
"         Selects all eligible IDocs → Reprocess button
" WE20 → partner profile: partner number, type, message, port
"         Missing partner profile → IDoc status 26 (partner not found)
" WE47 → status code reference list (51=app error, 53=success)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you find which ABAP function module processes an inbound IDoc?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Inbound IDoc process karne wala ABAP function module find karne ke liye, main WE57 check karta hu — IDoc message types ke liye function module assignment table. Message type aur basic type enter karo aur ye assigned processing FM dikhata hai. Alternatively, WE20 mein, partner profile inbound parameters mein process code hota hai, aur WE64 process codes ko unke processing function modules pe map karta hai. Identify hone ke baad, main FM ko SE37 mein check kar sakta hu, uski logic review kar sakta hu, aur status 51 kyu ho raha hai debug karne ke liye breakpoints set kar sakta hu.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To find the ABAP function module processing an inbound IDoc, I check WE57 — the function module assignment table for IDoc message types. Enter the message type and basic type and it shows the assigned processing FM. Alternatively, in WE20, the partner profile inbound parameters contain the process code, and WE64 maps process codes to their processing function modules. Once identified, I can check the FM in SE37, review its logic, and set breakpoints to debug why status 51 is occurring.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Debug karna ho toh: WE57 se FM name nikalo → SE37 mein FM kholo → external breakpoint lagao → WE19 se IDoc reprocess trigger karo → debugger mein step karo → exact line dekho jahan error aata hai. Yeh complete end-to-end debug flow hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Guessing the processing FM name or searching by keyword — use WE57 or WE64 which are the official configuration tables. Wrong FM identification wastes debugging time.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Finding IDoc processing FM:
" WE57 → enter Message type: ORDERS → Basic type: ORDERS05
"         → Execute → shows Function module name
" WE20 → partner number → Inbound parameters
"         → select message type row → Process code column
" WE64 → enter Process code → shows Function module mapped

" Example mapping:
" Message type: ORDERS → Process code: ORDE → FM: IDOC_INPUT_ORDERS
" Message type: MATMAS → Process code: MATM → FM: IDOC_INPUT_MATMAS

" Debug: SE37 → FM name → Breakpoints → External Breakpoint
" WE19 → reprocess IDoc → debugger opens in FM

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use WE19 to reprocess or test an IDoc?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: WE19 existing IDocs test ya reprocess karne ke liye use hota hai. Main IDoc document number enter karta hu, execute karta hu, aur WE19 IDoc content ko editable segment fields ke saath display karta hai — agar source system ne wrong values bhejey hain toh data directly correct karne deta hai. Fir main inbound processing ke liye Standard Task select karta hu aur Execute click karta hu — ye IDoc ko same processing pipeline ke through trigger karta hai. WE19 original ke based pe ek new test IDoc banata hai, isliye original IDoc record unchanged rehta hai. Reprocessing ke baad, main new IDoc ka status check karta hu — status 53 successful posting confirm karta hai, status 51 matlab issue persist karta hai aur zyada investigation chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: WE19 is used to test or reprocess existing IDocs. I enter the IDoc document number, execute, and WE19 displays the IDoc content with editable segment fields — allowing me to correct data directly if the source system sent wrong values. I then select Standard Task for inbound processing and click Execute — this triggers the IDoc through the same processing pipeline. WE19 creates a new test IDoc based on the original, so the original IDoc record remains unchanged. After reprocessing, I check the status of the new IDoc — status 53 confirms successful posting, status 51 means the issue persists and more investigation is needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: WE19 mein data modify karna — yeh production mein carefully karo. Agar external system ne wrong data bheja (e.g., wrong material number), WE19 mein correct karke test karo. But long term fix: external system ko correct data bhejna sikhao — WE19 manual workaround hai, permanent solution nahi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Modifying production IDoc data in WE19 without understanding why the original data was wrong — the external system will send the same wrong data again. Fix the source, not just the symptom.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WE19 reprocessing steps:
" WE19 → enter IDoc number (e.g., 0000000012345678)
" Execute → IDoc content screen opens

" Optional: modify segment data if source data was wrong
" Click on segment E1MARAM → change field value in right pane
" IMPORTANT: document why you modified — for audit trail

" Select processing type:
" Standard task: Inbound processing (normal repost)
" User defined function module: test specific FM directly
" Click Execute button

" After reprocess:
" WE02 → find new IDoc (similar timestamp) → check status
" Status 53 = successfully posted
" Status 51 = still failing, check new status record message

followupAnswerBank.question: What are IDoc partner profiles (WE20) and what happens if they are missing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: WE20 mein partner profiles har trading partner ke liye communication agreements define karte hain — specify karte hue partner kaunse message types bhej ya receive kar sakta hai, kaunsa port use karna hai, kaunsa processing function module message handle karta hai, aur error handling settings jaise alert generate karna hai ya status record. Agar partner profile missing ya incorrectly configured hai, inbound IDocs ko status 26 milta hai — Partner profile not found — aur outbound IDocs dispatch nahi ho sakte. Ye configuration issue hai, na ki code bug. Resolution mein WE20 mein partner profile create ya correct karna shaamil hai, jo typically ALE/EDI functional consultant ya Basis team karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Partner profiles in WE20 define the communication agreements for each trading partner — specifying which message types a partner can send or receive, which port to use, which processing function module handles the message, and error handling settings like whether to generate an alert or status record. If a partner profile is missing or incorrectly configured, inbound IDocs get status 26 — Partner profile not found — and outbound IDocs cannot be dispatched. This is a configuration issue, not a code bug. Resolution involves creating or correcting the partner profile in WE20, which is typically done by the ALE/EDI functional consultant or Basis team.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: New partner onboarding scenario: external system ne IDocs bhejne shuru kiye lekin sab status 26 pe fail ho rahe hain. WE20 mein us partner ka profile hi exist nahi karta — new configuration needed. Developer as support person escalate karo ALE consultant ko partner profile setup karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Trying to fix partner profile issues by modifying IDoc data — partner profile is configuration, not data. No amount of data correction fixes a missing partner profile. Configuration must be set up in WE20.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WE20 partner profile check:
" WE20 → Partner type: LI (vendor), LS (logical system), KU (customer)
" Enter partner number → check if profile exists
" If missing → IDoc status 26 in WE02

" WE20 profile components:
" Outbound parameters: message type + process code + port
" Inbound parameters: message type + process code + processing mode
"   Process mode: T=Transfer immediately, B=Background, D=Dialog

" Example partner profile entry:
" Partner number: 100234 (vendor)
" Partner type: LI
" Inbound: ORDERS05 → Process code: ORDE → Immed. processing
" Missing this → status 26 for all ORDERS IDocs from this vendor

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle outbound IDoc failures (status 02, 03, 68)?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jaanne ke liye outbound IDoc statuses: status 01 matlab IDoc database mein created. Status 02 matlab outbound processing ko passed. Status 03 matlab data port ko passed — dispatch karne ke liye ready. Status 12 matlab successfully receiver ko dispatched. Status 68 matlab dispatch ke dauran error — IDoc outbound port layer tak pohcha lekin deliver nahi ho saka. Status 68 ke liye, main receiving system ke liye SM59 RFC destination check karta hu, WE21 mein port configuration review karta hu, aur gateway-level errors ke liye SMGW check karta hu. Agar IDoc status 02 pe stuck hai, outbound processing trigger nahi hua hoga — main BD87 check karta hu ya WE14 ke through manually trigger karta hu.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Outbound IDoc statuses to know: status 01 means IDoc created in database. Status 02 means passed to outbound processing. Status 03 means data passed to port — ready to dispatch. Status 12 means successfully dispatched to receiver. Status 68 means error during dispatch — the IDoc reached the outbound port layer but could not be delivered. For status 68, I check the SM59 RFC destination for the receiving system, review the port configuration in WE21, and check SMGW for gateway-level errors. If the IDoc is stuck at status 02, the outbound processing may not have been triggered — I check BD87 or manually trigger via WE14.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Outbound IDoc status 68 production mein — external system ka receiver endpoint down tha. SMGW log mein 'connection refused' message. External team ne server restart kiya, phir WE14 se IDocs manually dispatch kiye — sab status 12 ho gaye.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming outbound IDoc issues are always ABAP code problems — often they are network, port, or receiver system issues. Check infrastructure first before code.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Outbound IDoc status flow:
" 01 → IDoc created
" 02 → in outbound queue / processing
" 03 → passed to port (ready to send)
" 12 → dispatched successfully to receiver
" 68 → dispatch error (network/port issue)

" WE02 → Direction: 1 (Outbound) → Status: 68 → Execute
" Status records show: 'Connection to partner refused'

" Fix options:
" WE21 → check port configuration and target address
" SM59 → test RFC connection to receiving system
" WE14 → manually trigger dispatch of waiting IDocs
" BD87 → mass reprocess outbound IDocs after fix
" SMGW → gateway log for detailed dispatch error

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you analyze IDoc segments to find data issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: IDoc segments hierarchical data containers hain — har segment type business document ke ek part se corresponding specific data fields hold karta hai. WE02 mein, IDoc pe click karne se two-pane view open hota hai: left pane segment tree structure dikhata hai, aur left pane mein kisi bhi segment pe click karne se uske field names aur values right pane mein display hote hain. Inbound IDoc data issues ke liye, main missing ya malformed values ke liye mandatory segment fields review karta hu. Main expected segment structure ko WE31 (IDoc segment definition) ke against cross-check karta hu confirm karne ke liye ki kaunse fields mandatory hain. Data quality issues — wrong material numbers, invalid date formats, missing vendor accounts — segment detail view mein clearly visible hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: IDoc segments are hierarchical data containers — each segment type holds specific data fields corresponding to a part of the business document. In WE02, clicking on an IDoc opens a two-pane view: the left pane shows the segment tree structure, and clicking any segment in the left pane displays its field names and values in the right pane. For inbound IDoc data issues, I review mandatory segment fields for missing or malformed values. I cross-check the expected segment structure against WE31 (IDoc segment definition) to confirm which fields are mandatory. Data quality issues — wrong material numbers, invalid date formats, missing vendor accounts — are clearly visible in the segment detail view.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Common data issue: external system bhej raha hai material number with leading zeros stripped — SAP expects 18-character padded material number. Segment mein dekha: E1EDPO2-IDTNR = '100234' instead of '000000000100234'. Conversion exit MATN1 handle karta hai yeh — but sometimes config gap hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Only reading the error message in status records without checking segment data. Sometimes the error message is generic but the actual cause is visible in the specific segment field values — both must be reviewed together.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" IDoc segment analysis in WE02:
" WE02 → click IDoc → left pane: segment tree
" E1ORDERS (control)
"   E1EDKA1 (partner data) ← click to see partner fields
"     E1EDP01 (line item 001) ← click to see item fields
"       E1EDP19 (product/material data) ← material number here

" Right pane shows:
" Field Name  | Field Value
" IDTNR       | 100234  ← material number sent by partner
" Expected    | 000000000100234  ← 18-char SAP material format

" WE31 → segment type E1EDP19 → field list with descriptions
" WE30 → IDoc type structure overview

followupAnswerBank.question: What is IDoc status 53 and how is it different from status 56?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Status 53 success state hai — Application Document Posted — matlab IDoc fully processed hua aur corresponding SAP document created hua: goods receipt ke liye ek material document, financial posting, ya for example sales order. Status 56 matlab IDoc with errors — IDoc processed hua lekin errors encounter hue, often partial processing ya exceptions wale document creation result karte hue. Status 56 ko status records review karne ki zarurat hoti hai samajhne ke liye ki error critical hai ya merely warning. Kuch status 56 scenarios certain configurations mein acceptable hain; doosre missed postings indicate karte hain jinhe reprocess karna chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Status 53 is the success state — Application Document Posted — meaning the IDoc was fully processed and a corresponding SAP document was created: a material document for goods receipt, a financial posting, or a sales order for example. Status 56 means IDoc with errors — the IDoc was processed but encountered errors, often resulting in partial processing or the creation of a document with exceptions. Status 56 requires reviewing the status records to understand whether the error is critical or merely a warning. Some status 56 scenarios are acceptable in certain configurations; others indicate missed postings that need reprocessing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Status 56 confusion production mein bahut hota hai. Functional team bolti hai 'it worked' but status 56 dikhta hai. Check karo: kya SAP document actually create hua? MB51 ya FB03 mein corresponding document hai ya nahi — document hai toh 56 acceptable warning tha. Document missing toh reprocess required.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Treating all status 56 IDocs as successful because some document creation occurred — always verify in the target application transaction (MB51 for goods receipt, FB03 for FI documents) that the expected document was fully and correctly created.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" IDoc status comparison:
" Status 53: Application Document Posted (full success)
"   → Material document created: check MB51
"   → FI posting created: check FB03
"   → Sales order created: check VA03

" Status 56: IDoc with errors (partial/warning)
"   → Check status records for error details
"   → Verify if target document was created
"   → If document missing: reprocess via WE19/BD87
"   → If document correct but warning: document for future ref

" ABAP check via EDIDC table:
SELECT docnum, status FROM edidc
  WHERE credat = @sy-datum AND mestyp = 'ORDERS'
  AND status IN ('51','56','53')
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_idocs).

support-debugging-007.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Authorization issue matlab user ko kuch karne ki permission nahi hai — jaise security guard ne roke diya gate pe. SAP mein yeh very systematic hai: har action ek Authorization Object se controlled hoti hai. SU53 matlab ek tool jo dikhata hai baar tumhara last failed authorization check kya tha — exactly kaunsa object missing tha, kaunsa field, kaunsa value required tha. Jaise ek rejected application form pe likha ho 'reason for rejection: signature missing on page 3.' Tum jaante ho exactly kya chahiye. SU53 use karna easy hai: user ko error aaya → SU53 kholne kaho immediately → screenshot lo ya khud jao check karo. IMPORTANT: SU53 sirf LAST failed check dikhata hai — agar ek session mein multiple auth failures aayein toh sirf aakhiri wala dikheaga. STAUTHTRACE iska solution hai — yeh powerful tool hai jo ek session ke SAARE authorization checks record karta hai: success wale bhi, fail wale bhi. STAUTHTRACE activate karo, user apna kaam karo, deactivate karo, analyze karo — filter karo 'failed checks only' — ek baar mein saari missing permissions ki list aa jaati hai. Phir PFCG mein role mein wo objects add karo. Authorization fix karna ek systematic process hai — guess mat karo, SU53 aur STAUTHTRACE se prove karo.
support-debugging-007.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Authorization troubleshooting ka complete workflow — production support mein yaise karo:

STEP 1 — SU53 IMMEDIATE: User ne error report kiya — 'Not authorized for this action.' Immediately SU53 kholne kaho ya khud user ke session mein jaake kholna. SU53 mein LAST failed authorization check dikhta hai. Yahan clearly dikhta hai:
- Authorization Object: jaise S_TCODE, M_MSEG_BWA, F_BKPF_BUK
- Field name: TCD, BWART, BUKRS
- Required Value: jo value check hui thi
- Existing authorizations: user ke paas kya tha

SU53 sirf current session ka last check dikhata hai. Agar user session close kar de ya naya session khole — data gone. Isliye IMMEDIATELY check karo.

STEP 2 — UNDERSTAND AUTH OBJECT: SU53 mein jo auth object dikhta hai uska matlab samjho:
- S_TCODE: Transaction code authorization — TCD field mein required T-code
- S_DEVELOP: Development authorization — object types SE38, SE37, etc
- M_MSEG_BWA: Movement type authorization for goods movement
- F_BKPF_BUK: FI posting authorization for company code
- P_ORGIN: HR organizational authorization
- S_RFC: RFC execution authorization

Har object ka documentation SU21 mein hai — field descriptions aur allowed values.

STEP 3 — STAUTHTRACE (when SU53 is not enough): STAUTHTRACE multiple auth failures trace karna ho ya background job ke liye:

a) STAUTHTRACE transaction kholo
b) 'Activate' button → user ID enter karo jiske liye trace chahiye → Activate
c) Ab wo user apna kaam kare (jo fail ho raha tha)
d) Wapas STAUTHTRACE → 'Deactivate'
e) 'Display' → results list
f) Filter: Authorization check result = 'Failed'
g) Ab saari missing auth objects ki list — ek baar mein complete picture

STEP 4 — PFCG ROLE UPDATE: Missing auth objects identify hone ke baad:
- Basis team ko role name batao (SU01 → user → Roles tab mein dekho)
- PFCG mein role open karo → Authorizations tab → change
- Missing auth object add karo, field values set karo
- Role generate (user compare)
- User reload (SU02 ya user re-login)

STEP 5 — VERIFY FIX: Auth change ke baad user se dobara same action karne kaho — confirm karo error gone. SU53 mein check karo — koi failed check nahi dikhna chahiye.

Common Auth Scenarios:
- New transaction deploy hua — S_TCODE mein T-code add karo
- New movement type use hua — M_MSEG_BWA extend karo
- New company code — F_BKPF_BUK extend karo
- RFC call unauthorized — S_RFC object check karo target system mein
- Background job failure — STAUTHTRACE on batch user
- HR data access — P_ORGIN object often complex
support-debugging-007.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'When a user reports an authorization error, I immediately ask them to open SU53 and take a screenshot — SU53 shows the last failed authorization check with the exact object, field, and required value. This tells me precisely what permission is missing. For more complex cases where multiple authorizations are missing, I use STAUTHTRACE — I activate it for the affected user, they reproduce the issue, I deactivate the trace and display results filtered by failed checks. This gives a complete list of all missing authorizations in one view. I then identify which role the user has assigned via SU01, open that role in PFCG, add the missing authorization objects with the required field values, generate the role, and have the user re-login to verify the fix.'

Hinglish tip: 'Real example ready rakho: User ko VA01 open nahi ho raha tha. SU53 se dekha — S_TCODE check failed, TCD = VA01 missing in user role. PFCG mein role khola, Authorization tab, S_TCODE object mein VA01 add kiya, role generate kiya. User re-login — VA01 open ho gaya. Simple case lekin systematic approach demonstrate karna important hai.'
support-debugging-007.codeExamples:
REPORT zdemo_auth_check.
" SU53: open immediately after error → shows last failed auth object
" STAUTHTRACE: activate → user reproduces → deactivate → display
DATA: lv_check TYPE sy-subrc.
" Explicit auth check in ABAP code
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000'  " Company code 1000
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.   " Activity: Create
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  " Auth failed — SU53 will show F_BKPF_BUK as last failed
  MESSAGE 'No authorization for company code 1000' TYPE 'E'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.
" Check S_TCODE (transaction authorization)
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_TCODE'
  ID 'TCD' FIELD 'FB50'.  " FI document entry
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'No authorization for transaction FB50' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.
" STAUTHTRACE shows ALL above checks — both pass and fail
" PFCG → role → Authorizations → add missing object + values

This tells me precisely what permission is missing. For more complex cases where multiple authorizations are missing, I use STAUTHTRACE — I activate it for the affected user, they reproduce the issue, I deactivate the trace and display results filtered by failed checks. This gives a complete list of all missing authorizations in one view. I then identify which role the user has assigned via SU01, open that role in PFCG, add the missing authorization objects with the required field values, generate the role, and have the user re-login to verify the fix.
support-debugging-007.interviewScriptHinglish: jab koi user authorization error report karta hai, main turant unhe SU53 open karke screenshot lene ko bolta hoon - SU53 last failed authorization check dikhata hai exact object, field aur required value ke saath. Ye precisely batata hai kya permission missing hai. More complex cases ke liye jahan multiple authorizations missing hain, main STAUTHTRACE use karta hoon - main affected user ke liye activate karta hoon, woh issue reproduce karte hain, main trace deactivate karta hoon aur failed checks filter karke results display karta hoon. Ye ek hi view mein sab missing authorizations ki complete list deta hai. Phir main SU01 ke through identify karta hoon ki user ko kaunsi role assigned hai, us role ko PFCG mein open karta hoon, required field values ke saath missing authorization objects add karta hoon, role generate karta hoon, aur user ko fix verify karne ke liye re-login karwata hoon.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Asking the user to reproduce the error and then check SU53 — if they reproduced it in a different transaction or same transaction triggered a different auth check, SU53 shows that check, not the original one.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Check SU53 right after the original error, before any other navigation.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Only using SU53 for background job authorization failures — background jobs run in batch, you cannot check SU53 in real time.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: STAUTHTRACE with the batch user ID is the correct approach for background job auth debugging.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Saving the role in PFCG without clicking Generate — the authorization profile is not updated and the runtime check still fails.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always generate after any authorization change. Also: adding overly broad values like '*' for all fields — security risk, always add specific required values only.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Adding only S_TCODE and not the other objects required within the transaction — the user can open the transaction but cannot perform specific actions.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: S_TCODE is necessary but not sufficient for full access.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Shows the user's last failed authorization check — fastest single-object diagnosis after an error.
System-wide trace of all authorization checks for a user/session — captures every pass and fail.
ABAP statement that validates a user against an authorization object and field values.
Standard FI authorization object protecting accounting document access by company code and activity.
Authorization object that controls which transactions a user is allowed to start.
Activity field on most auth objects — 01 create, 02 change, 03 display, 06 delete.
Role maintenance transaction — Basis adds missing objects and field values to user roles.
Return code after AUTHORITY-CHECK — 0 = allowed, 4 = not authorized, 8 = wrong field values.
User maintenance — assign roles, lock/unlock users, change validity for support investigations.
STAUTHTRACE filter that hides successful checks and shows the missing objects in one list.

support-debugging-007.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Authorization debugging identifies which auth object, field or value is missing when a user gets You are not authorized to do this errors.

Used for:
- Single failure diagnosis with SU53 right after the error occurs in the user's session.
- Multi-check diagnosis with STAUTHTRACE for batch users and complex transactions.
- Building the exact list of missing objects/field values to hand over to PFCG role owners.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- SU53, STAUTHTRACE, PFCG role maintenance, SU01 user master, SUIM role/user search.
- AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000' ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'. Check sy-subrc.
- Common objects: S_TCODE, S_TABU_DIS, S_DEVELOP, F_BKPF_BUK, V_VBAK_VKO.

Interview keyword: SU53 for one failure, STAUTHTRACE for the full picture.

followupAnswerBank.question: What exactly does SU53 show and why must you check it immediately?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU53 current user session mein most recent failed authorization check display karta hai. Ye Authorization Object name aur description, SAP jin required field values ke against check kar raha tha, aur unke roles se user ko currently assigned values dikhata hai. Comparison view instantly dikhata hai kya missing hai. Error ke turant baad SU53 check karna critical hai kyunki ye sirf last failed check retain karta hai — agar user kisi aur transaction pe navigate kare ya koi aur authorization check trigger ho, data overwrite ho jata hai aur evidence khoyi jaati hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU53 displays the most recent failed authorization check in the current user session. It shows the Authorization Object name and description, the required field values that SAP was checking against, and the values currently assigned to the user from their roles. The comparison view instantly shows what is missing. Checking SU53 immediately after the error is critical because it only retains the last failed check — if the user navigates to another transaction or triggers any other authorization check, the data is overwritten and the evidence is lost.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Phone pe user keh raha hai 'mujhe error aa raha hai' — pehli baat bolo: 'Abhi SU53 kholna mat karo, ek screenshot lo SU53 ka isi waqt.' Agar user ne kuch aur navigate kiya pehle, data gone. Urgency explain karo user ko.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Asking the user to reproduce the error and then check SU53 — if they reproduced it in a different transaction or same transaction triggered a different auth check, SU53 shows that check, not the original one. Check SU53 right after the original error, before any other navigation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU53 information layout:
" Failed Authorization Check:
"   Object: F_BKPF_BUK (FI: Acct Doc - Authorize Co. Code)
"   Field: BUKRS = 2000  ← required value (company code 2000)
"   Field: ACTVT = 01   ← required activity (create)

" User's Existing Authorizations (from assigned roles):
"   Role: Z_FI_POSTING
"   Object: F_BKPF_BUK
"   Field: BUKRS = 1000  ← only 1000, not 2000!
"   Field: ACTVT = 01,02  ← activities OK

" Clear gap: BUKRS 2000 missing in user role
" Fix: PFCG → role Z_FI_POSTING → add BUKRS 2000

followupAnswerBank.question: What is STAUTHTRACE and when do you use it instead of SU53?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: STAUTHTRACE ek traced session ke dauran saare authorization checks record karta hai — successful aur failed dono — traced activity ke liye complete authorization profile deta hai. SU53 quick single-failure diagnosis ke liye use karo jahan user ek specific error report kare aur tumhe immediately auth object chahiye. STAUTHTRACE tab use karo jab: multiple authorization failures exist ho sakte hain aur SU53 sirf last dikhata hai, issue ek background job mein hai jahan SU53 real time mein check nahi ho sakta, ya failure complex hai many steps ke saath aur tumhe saari required authorizations ek baar mein chahiye unhe together fix karne ke liye ek baar mein ek fix karke naye errors paane ke bajaye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: STAUTHTRACE records all authorization checks during a traced session — both successful and failed — providing a complete authorization profile for the traced activity. Use SU53 for quick single-failure diagnosis where the user reports one specific error and you need the auth object immediately. Use STAUTHTRACE when: multiple authorization failures may exist and SU53 only shows the last one, the issue is in a background job where SU53 cannot be checked in real time, or the failure is complex with many steps and you need all required authorizations at once to fix them together rather than fixing one at a time and getting new errors.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: STAUTHTRACE saves time in complex scenarios. Without it: fix SU53 auth → user tries again → new auth failure → SU53 again → fix → repeat 5 times. With STAUTHTRACE: all 5 missing auths visible at once → fix all at once → user tries → works first time. Efficiency bahut badh jaati hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Only using SU53 for background job authorization failures — background jobs run in batch, you cannot check SU53 in real time. STAUTHTRACE with the batch user ID is the correct approach for background job auth debugging.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" STAUTHTRACE step-by-step:
" 1. Transaction STAUTHTRACE
" 2. Enter user: BATCH_USER (or affected user)
" 3. Click Activate button
" 4. User: reproduce the failing action (or run batch job)
" 5. STAUTHTRACE → click Deactivate
" 6. Click Display → results table appears
" 7. Filter: Authorization check result = Failed
" 8. Results show ALL missing auth objects:
"    Object      | Field  | Required | Result
"    F_BKPF_BUK  | BUKRS  |   2000   | Failed
"    F_BKPF_GSB  | GSBER  |   0010   | Failed
"    M_MSEG_BWA  | BWART  |   101    | Failed
" 9. Fix all 3 at once in PFCG → one re-login → done

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you add a missing authorization in PFCG after identifying it via SU53?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU53 ya STAUTHTRACE ke through missing authorization object identify karne ke baad: pehle, SU01 mein Roles tab ke under user ke assigned roles check karo. PFCG mein appropriate role open karo. Authorizations tab pe jao aur Change click karo. Authorization tree mein, relevant authorization object find karo ya manually insert karo. SU53 ne jo missing dikhayi wo required field values add karo. Save karo aur fir Generate click karo — ye step authorization profile create karne ke liye mandatory hai. Finally, user ko compare karo ya unhe re-login karne do. Generate step skip karna matlab change role definition mein exist karta hai lekin runtime pe use hone wale authorization profile mein active nahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: After identifying the missing authorization object via SU53 or STAUTHTRACE: first, check the user's assigned roles in SU01 under the Roles tab. Open the appropriate role in PFCG. Go to the Authorizations tab and click Change. In the authorization tree, find the relevant authorization object or insert it manually. Add the required field values that SU53 showed were missing. Save and then click Generate — this step is mandatory to create the authorization profile. Finally, compare the user or have them re-login. Skipping the Generate step means the change exists in the role definition but is not active in the authorization profile used at runtime.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Common error in production support: auth object add kiya PFCG mein, save kiya — lekin Generate button press nahi kiya. User ko still error aa raha hai. Rule: always generate after every auth change. Generate se profile create hota hai jo actual runtime check use karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Saving the role in PFCG without clicking Generate — the authorization profile is not updated and the runtime check still fails. Always generate after any authorization change. Also: adding overly broad values like '*' for all fields — security risk, always add specific required values only.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" PFCG role update workflow:
" SU01 → user ID → Roles tab → note role name
" PFCG → role name → Display/Change
" Authorizations tab → Change (pencil icon)
" Auth object tree:
"   Cross-application authorization objects
"     → F_BKPF_BUK (if FI auth missing)
"       → BUKRS: add value 2000
"       → ACTVT: values 01, 02, 03
" Save → IMPORTANT: click Generate button
" Generate creates authorization profile ZROLE_xxx
" User comparison: PFCG → User tab → compare users
" OR user re-login to pick up new profile
" Verify: user performs action → no more auth error

followupAnswerBank.question: What is authorization object S_TCODE and how does it differ from other auth objects?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: S_TCODE transaction-level authorization object hai — ye control karta hai ki user specific transaction code start kar bhi sakta hai ya nahi. Iska ek field hai: TCD (transaction code). Agar S_TCODE check fail kare, transaction entry pe block ho jaati hai aur transaction ke andar koi aur authorization checks execute nahi hote. Doosre objects jaise F_BKPF_BUK (FI company code), M_MSEG_BWA (goods movement type), ya M_MATE_STA (material status) S_TCODE pass hone ke baad transactions ke andar specific actions control karte hain. SU53 mein, agar tum S_TCODE ko failed object ke roop mein dekho, fix hai user ke role mein PFCG mein transaction code add karna.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: S_TCODE is the transaction-level authorization object — it controls whether a user can even start a specific transaction code. It has one field: TCD (transaction code). If S_TCODE check fails, the transaction is blocked at entry and no other authorization checks within the transaction execute. Other objects like F_BKPF_BUK (FI company code), M_MSEG_BWA (goods movement type), or M_MATE_STA (material status) control specific actions within transactions after S_TCODE has passed. In SU53, if you see S_TCODE as the failed object, the fix is to add the transaction code to the user's role in PFCG.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: S_TCODE failure is the most common and simplest auth issue — new transaction deployed, user role not updated. Quick fix: PFCG → role → add T-code to S_TCODE object → generate. More complex objects like P_ORGIN (HR) or B_BUPA_RLT (Business Partner) are harder — require functional team input on correct values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Adding only S_TCODE and not the other objects required within the transaction — the user can open the transaction but cannot perform specific actions. S_TCODE is necessary but not sufficient for full access.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" S_TCODE in ABAP — implicit check when T-code is entered:
" SAP automatically checks S_TCODE when user runs any T-code
" No explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK needed for S_TCODE in most cases

" But can be checked explicitly:
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_TCODE'
  ID 'TCD' FIELD 'VA01'.  " Check sales order T-code
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'No access to VA01' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

" SU53 showing S_TCODE failure:
" Object: S_TCODE | Field: TCD | Required: VA01
" Fix: PFCG → role → S_TCODE → add VA01 to TCD field
" OR: add via menu-based role maintenance

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you trace authorization failures for a background batch job?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background jobs ek technical service user ke under run karte hain — main background execution ke liye real time mein SU53 check nahi kar sakta. Standard approach STAUTHTRACE hai: job trigger karne se pehle batch user ID ke liye trace activate karo, immediate execution ke liye job ko foreground mein ya SM36 ke through manually run karo, fir deactivate karo aur STAUTHTRACE results display karo failed checks se filtered. Saari missing authorizations ek view mein appear hoti hain. SM37 job log mein authorization failure messages dikhane wale ke liye, STAUTHTRACE object-level detail deta hai jo generic job log message nahi deta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Background jobs run under a technical service user — I cannot check SU53 in real time for background execution. The standard approach is STAUTHTRACE: activate trace for the batch user ID before triggering the job, run the job manually in foreground or via SM36 for immediate execution, then deactivate and display STAUTHTRACE results filtered by failed checks. All missing authorizations appear in one view. For SM37 job log showing authorization failure messages, the STAUTHTRACE provides the object-level detail that the generic job log message does not.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek aur approach: job jo fail ho rahi hai usse SE38 mein same variant se apne user ID se run karo — agar tumhare paas bhi auth nahi hai toh SU53 se seedha dekh sakte ho. Lekin agar tumhare paas access hai lekin batch user ko nahi — STAUTHTRACE is the only way.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Running SU53 for your own user ID to diagnose batch job failures — you likely have broader authorizations than the restricted batch user. Always trace or check using the actual batch user ID.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Background job auth debugging workflow:
" 1. Note batch user: SM37 → failed job → General data tab
"    Technical user: BATCH_USER_FI

" 2. STAUTHTRACE → Activate → Enter user: BATCH_USER_FI
" 3. SM36 → schedule same program/variant → run immediately
"    OR: SE38 → same program → run with F8 (in foreground)
" 4. STAUTHTRACE → Deactivate → Display
" 5. Filter: Check result = Failed
" 6. All missing auth objects shown:
"    F_BKPF_BUK / BUKRS = 3000 / Failed
"    F_BKPF_KOA / KOART = D,K  / Failed
" 7. PFCG → batch user's role → add missing objects → generate

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between SU01, SU21, SU53, and PFCG?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU01 user master maintenance transaction hai — users create karna, roles assign karna, passwords reset karna, accounts lock aur unlock karna. SU21 authorization object definition repository hai — har authorization object ki technical definition including uske fields aur allowed values contain karta hai, samajhne ke liye useful ki auth object kya control karta hai. SU53 authorization check analysis tool hai — current user session ke liye last failed authorization check dikhata hai, diagnosis ke liye use hota hai. PFCG role management ke liye Profile Generator hai — authorization roles create karna, roles ke andar authorization objects aur field values maintain karna, aur authorization profiles generate karna jo runtime checks use karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU01 is the user master maintenance transaction — create users, assign roles, reset passwords, lock and unlock accounts. SU21 is the authorization object definition repository — contains the technical definition of each authorization object including its fields and allowed values, useful for understanding what an auth object controls. SU53 is the authorization check analysis tool — shows the last failed authorization check for the current user session, used for diagnosis. PFCG is the Profile Generator for role management — create authorization roles, maintain authorization objects and field values within roles, and generate the authorization profiles that runtime checks use.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Flow: SU53 mein problem identify karo → SU01 mein user ka role dekho → PFCG mein role mein fix karo → SU01 se user comparison karo. SU21 use karo jab naya custom auth object create karna ho ya existing object ka meaning samajhna ho.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Confusing SU01 and PFCG — SU01 assigns roles to users but does not define what the role contains. PFCG defines the role content. To fix an auth issue, PFCG is needed, not SU01.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SAP Auth transactions and their purpose:
" SU01 → User master: create/modify user, assign roles, passwords
"         SU01 → enter user → Roles tab → see assigned roles
" SU21 → Auth object catalog: field names, documentation
"         SU21 → F_BKPF_BUK → shows BUKRS + ACTVT fields
" SU53 → Diagnosis: last failed auth check for current session
"         SU53 → execute immediately after error
" PFCG → Role maintenance: define role content and auth values
"         PFCG → role → Authorizations → add/modify objects

" Workflow:
" SU53 → identify object → SU01 user → role name → PFCG fix
" PFCG → generate → SU01 user compare → re-login → verify

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SUIM and how is it useful for authorization analysis?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SUIM User Information System hai — SAP authorization landscape ke liye ek comprehensive reporting aur search tool. Ye users ko assigned roles, transaction code access, authorization object values, aur combinations se search karne deta hai. Key use cases: audit purposes ke liye specific transaction tak access wale saare users dhundhna, kaunse roles mein specific authorization object hai dhundhna, identify karna kaunse users specific company code mein post kar sakte hain, aur segregation of duties violations detect karna — wo users jinke pas documents create karne aur approve karne dono ke access hai. SUIM kuch modify nahi karta — ye read-only analysis aur audit tool hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SUIM is the User Information System — a comprehensive reporting and search tool for the SAP authorization landscape. It allows searching users by assigned roles, by transaction code access, by authorization object values, and by combinations. Key use cases: finding all users with access to a specific transaction for audit purposes, finding which roles contain a specific authorization object, identifying users who can post to a specific company code, and detecting segregation of duties violations — users with both access to create and approve documents. SUIM does not modify anything — it is a read-only analysis and audit tool.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Audit request: 'Who can access FB50 (FI document posting) in production?' — SUIM → Users by Complex Criteria → Transaction FB50 → Execute → complete list. Send to auditors. Much faster than manually checking every user's roles.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Trying to find all users with a transaction by checking each user's SU01 profile manually — extremely inefficient. SUIM provides this cross-user analysis in seconds.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SUIM navigation for common queries:
" SUIM → Users by Complex Selection Criteria
"   → Roles → enter role name: Z_FI_POSTING
"   → Execute → shows all users with this role

" SUIM → Users by Transaction Code
"   → Transaction: FB50
"   → Execute → all users who can access FB50

" SUIM → Roles by Complex Criteria
"   → Authorization object: F_BKPF_BUK
"   → Execute → all roles containing this object

" SUIM → Roles by Transaction Code
"   → Transaction: VA01
"   → Execute → all roles that grant VA01 access
"   → Useful to identify which role to add to user

followupAnswerBank.question: How does AUTHORITY-CHECK work in ABAP code and what does SY-SUBRC return?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AUTHORITY-CHECK runtime authorization verification ke liye ABAP statement hai. Ye authorization object name aur ek ya zyada field-value pairs specify karta hai check karne ke liye. Execution ke baad, SY-SUBRC = 0 matlab user authorized hai — saare specified field values user ke authorization profile se match karte hain. SY-SUBRC = 4 matlab not authorized — required field values user ke assigned roles mein nahi hain. SY-SUBRC = 8 matlab authorization object user ke authorization buffer mein maintained nahi hai — typically configuration gap. Jab SY-SUBRC 4 hai, wo AUTHORITY-CHECK wahi hai jo SU53 last failed check ke roop mein display karega.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AUTHORITY-CHECK is the ABAP statement for runtime authorization verification. It specifies the authorization object name and one or more field-value pairs to check. After execution, SY-SUBRC = 0 means the user is authorized — all specified field values match the user's authorization profile. SY-SUBRC = 4 means not authorized — the required field values are not in the user's assigned roles. SY-SUBRC = 8 means the authorization object is not maintained in the user's authorization buffer — typically a configuration gap. When SY-SUBRC is 4, that AUTHORITY-CHECK is what SU53 will display as the last failed check.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Custom ABAP program mein authorization check add karna best practice hai — sensitive data access pe, delete operations pe, posting transactions pe. Generic programs without auth checks are security risks. Any custom program accessing company financial data should have F_BKPF_BUK check minimum.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not checking SY-SUBRC after AUTHORITY-CHECK — the program continues processing even when the user is not authorized, creating a security vulnerability. Always check SY-SUBRC = 0 before proceeding with protected operations.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" AUTHORITY-CHECK syntax and SY-SUBRC handling
REPORT zdemo_auth_check.

" Single field check
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_TCODE'
  ID 'TCD' FIELD 'ZSD_ORDER'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.  " 4 = no auth, 8 = object not in buffer
  MESSAGE 'Not authorized for ZSD_ORDER' TYPE 'E'. RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Multi-field auth check
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_company_code  " dynamic value
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.            " create activity
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  " Proceed with FI posting
ELSEIF sy-subrc = 4.
  MESSAGE |No auth for company { lv_company_code }| TYPE 'E'.
ELSEIF sy-subrc = 8.
  MESSAGE 'Auth object not maintained' TYPE 'W'.
ENDIF.
" SU53 shows this object if sy-subrc was 4
